seoTitle: How to Reduce PDF File Size in Canva Easily
metaDescription: Compress large PDF files in Canva so documents upload and email faster without noticeably losing visual quality.
intro: Oversized PDFs can slow down emails and uploads, which becomes a real problem when you're sending documents regularly. Canva includes a way to shrink PDF file size while keeping the design readable. This guide covers accessing that feature and getting a smaller file out the other end, useful for anyone managing business documents or reports.

steps.heading: Access the PDF compression option
steps.body: From your Canva document, find the download or export settings where file size options appear.

steps.heading: Choose a smaller file size setting
steps.body: Select the compressed or smaller PDF option instead of the standard high-resolution export.

steps.heading: Review the output quality
steps.body: Open the compressed PDF to confirm the text and images still look acceptable for your purpose.

steps.heading: Share or upload the smaller file
steps.body: Use the reduced PDF for email attachments or web uploads where file size limits matter.

faq.question: Does compressing a PDF in Canva reduce text quality?
faq.answer: Text generally stays sharp; it's images and graphics that see the most size reduction and any visible quality trade-off.

faq.question: Can I compress a PDF that wasn't originally made in Canva?
faq.answer: This feature is built around PDFs created or uploaded within Canva's own workflow, so results may vary with outside files.

faq.question: Is there a file size limit for uploads after compression?
faq.answer: Smaller files are generally easier to email or upload since most platforms cap attachment sizes, which compression helps you stay under.
